meaning: vt.掌握,控制,支配vi.统治,成为主流,支配,占优势
examples: Very little light filters through the canopy of leaves and branches in a rain forest to reach ground level—or close to the ground—and at those levels the yellow-to-green wavelengths predominate.
Private interest was not allowed to predominate over the public good.
In older age groups women predominate because men tend to die younger.
The love songs that predominate, as they usually do with Oberst, are long on social context.
For most of the time since 1997, the first interpretation has appeared to predominate.
